description_long: This is a small, historic stone church with a rectangular nave and a semicircular apse at the rear. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, giving it a rustic and ancient appearance. The roof is covered with reddish-orange terracotta tiles, and a simple stone chimney rises from one side. Ivy and vegetation partially climb the walls, especially near the apse and along the side, adding to its aged character. The church features a small arched window on the side and a modest entrance door at the front, with a belfry containing two arched openings above the main entrance. The overall structure is compact, with minimal ornamentation, reflecting a Romanesque architectural style.
description: A small, rustic stone church with a terracotta-tiled roof, semicircular apse, and a simple belfry above the entrance. Ivy climbs the weathered stone walls, and the building exudes a historic Romanesque charm.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the church with a single arched window, the stone chimney, and the terracotta-tiled roof, as well as some ivy climbing the wall.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the semicircular apse, the belfry with two arched openings, and dense ivy growth at the base of the apse.
description_view_2: This side view displays the stone chimney, the apse, and extensive ivy covering the lower wall, with the roof tiles visible from above.
description_view_3: The front view reveals the main entrance door, the belfry above with two arched openings, and a mostly plain stone facade with some vegetation at the base.
